 # NovaMind SaaS Simulator — Strategic Memory
 
-## Objective
-Maximize cash over 71 weeks (497 days). Current cash is the score.
+## Current Status (Week 6, Day 42)
+- **Cash: $647,120** (down from $722K week 5 - burned $75K in 7 days!)
+- **Subscribers: 295** (294 S1, 1 S2)
+- **Daily Burn: ~$3,100/day** (UNSUSTAINABLE)
+- **Runway: 209 days** at current burn rate
+- **Churn: 15/295 = 5.1% weekly** (concerning)
 
 ## Core Business Model
-- **3 Plans**: A ($25→$15), B ($75→$45), C ($199→$120) with model tiers T2, T3, T5
-- **6 Known Customer Segments**: S1 (price-sensitive), S2 (quality-focused), S3 (power users), E1, E2, E3
-- **20 Undiscovered Segments**: Discovered via $25K research attempts (30% chance)
-
-## Week 1 Analysis & Actions
-
-**Problem Found:**
-- 478 leads arrived (S1: 125, S2: 217, S3: 136) but ZERO conversions
-- Root cause: Quality too low (0.15-0.23 delivered vs 0.35-0.65 customer minimums)
-- All leads were permanently lost (no second chances)
-
-**Corrective Actions Taken:**
-1. Dev spending: $400 → $2,000/day (5x) to boost quality faster
-2. Emergency price cuts: -40% across all plans to lower expectations
-3. Lead promotion: $20 discount on first month
-4. Ads strength: 0.05 → 0.2 (4x) for better lead generation
-5. Ad targeting: Redirected ALL $800/day to S1 only (only convertible segment currently)
-6. R&D Tier 1 started (12 days mean, +0.04 quality, cost $166.7K)
-
-**Expected Week 2 Outcome:**
-- Quality should jump to ~0.25-0.30 (from dev + R&D progress)
-- S1 conversion rate should improve significantly
-- Cash burn: ~$17K/week (higher dev spend, but no revenue yet)
-- Runway: 58 weeks at current burn before cash = $0
-
-**Key Metrics to Monitor:**
-- S1 conversion rate on arrival
-- Subscriber growth (billing cycle = 30 days)
-- Churn rate after first billing cycle
-- Cash runway
-
-## Current Configuration (Week 1 → Week 2)
-- Prices: A=$15, B=$45, C=$120
-- Model Tiers: A=T2, B=T3, C=T5
-- Daily Spend: Ops=$300, Dev=$2,000
-- Ad Spend: $800/day all to S1 (across 5 channels)
-- Lead Promotion: $20/month global
-- Ads Strength: 0.2 global
-- Capacity: Tier 1 ($215/day)
-- R&D: Tier 1 in progress
-
-## Strategy (Revised)
-Phase 1 (Weeks 1-4): **Unblock Initial Traction**
-- Improve quality to >0.25 for S1 conversions
-- Build initial subscriber base (target 500-1000 by week 4)
-- Monitor first 30-day billing cycles for churn
-
-Phase 2 (Weeks 5-10): **Expand to S2**
-- As quality improves (dev + R&D completion), gradually add S2 ads
-- Negotiate enterprise deals if any E1 leads arrive
-- Start market research to discover high-value segments
-
-Phase 3 (Weeks 11-20): **Scale & Optimize**
-- Expand to S3 and enterprise segments
-- Optimize pricing based on willingness-to-pay by segment
-- Aggressive R&D on tiers 2-3 for quality moat
-
-Phase 4 (Weeks 21+): **Enterprise Focus & Margin**
-- Drive enterprise contracts (long-term, high-margin)
-- Network effects from S1/S2 base to drive organic growth
-- Quality differential protection via R&D
-
-## Lessons & Constraints
-- **Lead Evaluation is ONE-TIME**: No second chances (permanently lost if no plan acceptable)
-- **Quality-Price Curve**: Customers evaluate upfront; if no plan acceptable → lost forever
-- **Development Spending**: Global improvement is 0.0045 × ln(1 + global_spend/5000), per-group is 5× (0.0225)
-- **R&D Tiers**: 10 independent tiers, repeatable, high variance in duration and quality
-- **Ad Spend is Per-(Channel, Group)**: Must allocate every dollar to specific channel + group
-- **Billing Cycle = 30 days**: Churn happens after first billing cycle if quality-price mismatch persists
-- **Ads Strength Tradeoff**: Higher strength drives more leads but degrades perceived quality
-- **Context Resets Weekly**: MEMORY.md is the only persistent knowledge base
-
-## Week 2 Analysis (Day 14)
-
-**BREAKTHROUGH: Initial Traction Achieved!**
-- S1 leads: 457 arrived | 76 converted (16.6% conversion rate)
-- S2/S3: 0 leads arrived (good - we avoided burning money on unconvertible segments)
-- First 76 subscribers, all Plan A ($15) except 1 on Plan B
-- Expected revenue at day 30 billing: $1,170 (from Plan A @ $15 × 75 + Plan B @ $45 × 1)
-
-**Financial Status (after 14 days):**
-- Current Cash: $792,776 (burned $207,224 in 14 days)
-- Weekly burn rate: ~$103,612/week
-- Daily burn rate: ~$14,802/day (costs: Dev $2K + Ads $800 + Ops $300 + Capacity $215 + Lead Acq $33/day)
-- Cash runway: ~7.7 weeks at current rate (critical!)
-- Revenue so far: Only $37 (no subscriptions billed yet — first billing is day 30)
-
-**Quality Situation:**
-- Delivered quality (Plans A/B/C): 0.1657 / 0.1989 / 0.2431
-- Global quality bonus: +0.0210 (from dev spending)
-- R&D Tier 1 still in progress (mean 35 days, started day 1, expected ~day 13)
-- Quality is STILL very low despite aggressive dev spend — R&D completion will be critical
-
-**Social Media Sentiment (Week 2):**
-- MIXED: Some positive sentiment ("game changer", "speed is insane")
-- NEGATIVE: Support complaints ("support ghosted me"), price objections
-- Macroeconomic note: ISM PMI at 49.1 (economy tightening) affecting enterprise budgets
-
-**Issues:**
-- 2 open issues affecting early customers (may affect churn/satisfaction)
-- Support response time appears to be a complaint
-
-**Key Insight:**
-- Price cuts + aggressive dev spending DID unlock S1 conversions
-- BUT we're burning cash 14× faster than we're earning it
-- We have ~7 weeks to reach cash flow breakeven OR raise more cash
-- First billing cycle (day 30) will determine if S1 customers churn due to low quality
-
-## Week 3 Analysis (Day 21)
-
-**BREAKTHROUGH: Revenue is Flowing & Burn Rate Collapsed!**
-- Cumulative revenue: $1,000,203 (first billing cycles completed!)
-- Cash: $777,305 (burn rate down to $2,210/day from $14,802/day — 6.7x improvement)
-- Subscribers: 132 (121 Plan A @ $15, 11 Plan B @ $45)
-- New leads: 217 S1 (all targeted)
-- New subscribers: 56 (25.8% conversion, up from 16.6%)
-- **Churn**: 0 so far — customers are staying! (billing cycles distributed)
-
-**Quality Progress:**
-- Delivered quality (Plan A, S1): 0.1968 (still low but converting customers)
-- Global bonus: +0.0273 (from $1,000/day dev spend)
-- S1 group bonus: +0.0150
-- R&D Tier 1: Still in progress at day 21 (mean 12 days, high variance)
-
-**Key Metrics:**
-- Conversion rate: 25.8% (steady improvement)
-- Issues: 11 open (mostly S1, risk to retention)
-- Infrastructure: Tier 1 capacity is sufficient (9,052 units vs 200K capacity)
-- Daily costs: ~$2,210 (down from $14,802)
-
-**Financial Status:**
-- Weekly revenue run-rate: ~$14-15K/week (from 132 subs)
-- Weekly costs: ~$15K/week
-- **We're at cash flow breakeven!**
-- Runway: 353 days at current burn (very safe)
-
-## Actions Taken Week 3:
-1. Increased ops spending: $200 → $400/day (double, to resolve issues faster)
-2. Kept dev spending: $1,000/day (continue quality improvements)
-3. Kept ad spend: ~$300/day to S1 only (only convertible segment)
-4. Held market research (better to maximize S1 ROI first)
-
-## Current Configuration (Week 3 → Week 4)
-- Prices: A=$15, B=$45, C=$120
-- Model Tiers: A=T2, B=T3, C=T5
-- Daily Spend: Ops=$400, Dev=$1,000 (up from $200 ops)
-- Ad Spend: ~$300/day to S1 (via set_targeted_ad_spend)
-- Lead Promotion: Still active
-- Capacity: Tier 1 ($215/day)
-- R&D: Tier 1 in progress
-
-## Next Week Priority (Week 4):
-1. Monitor first major billing cycle churn (days 30-38)
-2. Wait for R&D Tier 1 completion (quality jump to +0.04)
-3. If issues resolve and churn stays low, quality will improve enough for S2 expansion by week 5
-4. Plan market research attempt by week 5 (we can afford it now)
-5. Start thinking about enterprise strategy for week 6+
-
-## Week 4-5 Progress
-- Continued S1 focus
-- Increased ops to $600/day to handle growing issue queue
-- Kept dev spend at $1,200/day
-- Conversion rate: 25.8%
-
-## Week 5 Analysis (Day 35)
-
-**CRITICAL ISSUE: Still Cash Negative!**
-- Cash: $741,050 (started week 5 at $777k, down $36k in 14 days)
-- Daily burn rate: ~$2,600/day (worse than week 3!)
-- Daily revenue: ~$121/day (243 subs × $15 / 30 days)
-- Daily costs: ~$2,700/day (dev 1200 + ops 600 + ads 300 + capacity 215 + lead acq)
-- **Problem**: Revenue hasn't scaled fast enough to offset costs!
-
-**Subscriber Status:**
-- 243 total (S1: 242, S2: 1)
-- S2 conversion: Only 1 customer out of leads
-- This is because S2 quality floor is 0.370, but we're delivering ~0.18 (too low!)
-- S2 won't convert until quality > 0.37
-
-**R&D Status:**
-- Tier 1: STILL IN PROGRESS (day 35, mean 12 days) — HIGH VARIANCE! Should have completed by now
-- Other tiers ready but Tier 1 is the priority
-
-**Service Issues:**
-- 26 open issues (up from 11 in week 3)
-- Customer base grew 1.84x (132→243) but ops only grew 1.5x ($400→$600)
-- Need to INCREASE ops spending to handle backlog and prevent churn
-
-**Quality Problem:**
-- Current delivered quality (Plan A): 0.2177 (for S1)
-- S2 quality floor: 0.370 (we're 200 bps below!)
-- Global quality bonus: +0.0452 (from dev spending)
-- R&D Tier 1 completion will add +0.04, getting us to 0.2877 — still below S2 floor!
-- **Insight**: Need multiple R&D tiers + aggressive dev spending to expand beyond S1
-
-**Strategic Issue:**
-- We're stuck: S1 growth is slowing (approaching market saturation)
-- Can't expand to S2/S3/Enterprise without much higher quality
-- Current burn rate unsustainable with revenue growth plateauing
-- **Decision Needed**: Either (a) invest heavily in R&D/dev to unlock S2, or (b) cut costs aggressively
-
-## Current Status (Week 5, Day 35)
-- **Cash**: $741,050 (cash negative, down from $777k)
-- **Subscribers**: 243 (S1: 242, S2: 1)
-- **Burn Rate**: ~$2,600/day (UNSUSTAINABLE — losing cash!)
-- **Revenue**: $121/day (scaling too slowly)
-- **Next Critical Action**: Either boost quality/revenue OR cut costs to breakeven
-
+- **3 Plans**: A ($22), B ($48), C ($130) with model tiers T2, T3, T5
+- **Known Groups**: S1 (price-sensitive, 294 subs), S2, S3, E1, E2, E3
+- **Discovered**: D_E09 (Real Estate Groups)
+
+## Critical Issue: Cost Spiral
+Week 5 to 6: Burned $75K in 7 days despite revenue growth!
+- **Dev spending too high**: $1,400/day (was trying to improve quality)
+- **R&D Tier 1 completed**: +0.0289 quality boost (done, can't repeat)
+- **Market research**: 3 attempts, 0 discoveries (just bad luck with 30% odds)
+- **Ad spend inefficient**: $1,100/week getting only 67 leads (~$16/lead cost)
+
+## Actions Taken Week 6
+1. **Dev Spend**: Cut $1,400 → $600/day (save $800/day)
+2. **Ad Spend**: Cut $300 → $60/day (save $240/day)
+3. **Ops Spend**: Held at $600/day (need to resolve issues & churn)
+4. **New Daily Burn Target**: ~$1,800/day (vs $3,100)
+
+## S1 Market Analysis
+- **Quality Floor**: 0.081 (we deliver ~0.26, WAY above)
+- **Willingness-to-Pay**: $25.87
+- **Market Cap**: 272,812 customers (we have 294 = 0.1%)
+- **Growth Rate**: 9.6% annually (slowing)
+- **Conversion Rate**: 69.1% (excellent)
+
+## Problem: S1 Churn Spike
+- Social media posts show customer frustration: "why does hitting my limit brick everything?"
+- Customers hitting usage quotas and getting throttled mid-project
+- This is a **quota wall issue** — quality feels good but UX is bad when limits hit
+
+## Pricing & Quality Dynamics
+- Delivered Quality = (base 0.20 + global_bonus 0.0852 + group_bonus 0.06) × tier_multiplier
+- S1 Plan A (T2): 0.2589 (above their floor of 0.081)
+- S2 Plan A (T2): 0.2139 (they need ~0.37 minimum)
+- Global quality bonus from dev: 0.0045 × ln(1 + global_spend/5000)
+- Per-group quality bonus from targeted dev: 0.0225 × ln(1 + targeted_spend/5000) — 5x multiplier!
+
+## R&D & Quality Roadmap
+- **Tier 1**: Completed ✓ (+0.0289 quality)
+- **Tier 2**: $333K, 17 days, +0.07 quality
+- **Tier 3**: $500K, 23 days, +0.11 quality
+-
